entries.definition:
ʿ–y–l (q.v.), particularly in the associated concepts of ‘to
have many children’. Of this root, only تَعُولُوا taʿūlū occurs once in
the Qur’an.

تَعُول taʿūl [imperf. of v. intrans. عَال ʿāla] to deviate, to stray
from the correct path, to be biased (4:3) فَإِنْ خِفْتُمْ أَلَّا تَعْدِلُوا فَوَاحِدَةً أَوْ مَا
مَلَكَتْ أَيْمَانُكُمْ ذَلِكَ أَدْنَى أَلَّا تَعُولُوا but if you fear that you cannot be
equitable, then [marry only] one, or [turn to] [lit. what your right
hands own] your slaves, in this way you are less likely to deviate
[from the proper way] (see ع/ي/ل ʿ–y–l).
entries.definition_english:
ʿ–y–l (q.v.), particularly in the associated concepts of ‘to
have many children’. Of this root, only تَعُولُوا taʿūlū occurs once in
the Qur’an.

تَعُول taʿūl [imperf. of v. intrans. عَال ʿāla] to deviate, to stray
from the correct path, to be biased (4:3) فَإِنْ خِفْتُمْ أَلَّا تَعْدِلُوا فَوَاحِدَةً أَوْ مَا
مَلَكَتْ أَيْمَانُكُمْ ذَلِكَ أَدْنَى أَلَّا تَعُولُوا but if you fear that you cannot be
equitable, then [marry only] one, or [turn to] [lit. what your right

[from the proper way] (see ع/ي/ل ʿ–y–l).

entries.definition:
ʿ–y–l children, family, to have many children; to be underfed,
to be dependent, to be poor; to deviate; to be conceited, to walk
with a conceited gait. There is a certain degree of overlapping
between this root and the root ع/و/ل ʿ–w–l (q.v.), whereby the
sense of ‘to have many children’ might be derived from either. Of
this root, two forms, maybe three, occur once each in the Qur’an:
عَائِلًا ʿāʾilan; عَيْلَة ʿaylatan (and, possibly, تَعُولُوا taʿūlū).

عَائِل ʿāʾil [act. part.] one who is in straitened circumstances,
one who is needy, impoverished (93:8) وَوَجَدَكَ عَائِلًا فَأَغْنَى and did
He not find you in need and make you self-sufficient?

عَيْلَة ʿaylatun [v. n./n.] being in straitened conditions, poverty,
want (9:28) وَإِنْ خِفْتُمْ عَيْلَةً فَسَوْفَ يُغْنِيكُمُ اللَّهُ مِنْ فَضْلِهِ إِنْ شَاءَ and if you fear
want, God will enrich you of His bounty, if He wishes.

تَعُول taʿūl [imperf. of v. intrans. عَال ʿāla] to have too many
children (in an interpretation of 4:3) ذَلِكَ أَدْنَى أَلَّا تَعُولُوا that is nearer
(i.e., more likely) [cause] for your not having [too] many children
(see ع/و/ل ʿ–w–l).
